target.name: Asimov
target.founder: Alec Nielsen, Chris Voigt, Douglas Densmore, Raja Srinivas

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $800M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Asimov, a Series B biotech company, is valued at $800M post-money following $209M in total fundraising. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 3 public peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private companies in the Biotech & Health sector, with 4 direct stage-matched private comparables used for triangulation.

Two valuation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $800M base valuation, implies $1.5B. Peer-median anchoring across four same-sector, same-stage companies (Eikon Therapeutics at $3.3B, Neko Health at $1.8B, New Limit at $810M, and Profluent at $600M) yields a median valuation of $1.3B, with a range of $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp P/S multiples (6.6x median across the three public tickers) could not be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue figures for the private target.

Triangulation of the two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $1.1B (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median estimate of $1.4B. This implies modest appreciation from the current $800M post-money. Key uncertainty centers on Asimov's revenue trajectory and relative positioning within the wide $600M–$3.3B peer dispersion, reflecting material heterogeneity in biotech Series B valuations.
